title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle to intensify before hitting NT | ABC NEWS
description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle has entered the Gulf of Carpenteria and is expected to intensify as it approaches the Northern Territory. In Queensland, attention is turning to restoring power and assessing damage after the system crossed on Friday. Weather presenter Nate Byrne reports from the town of Weipa on the west coast of the Cape York Peninsula. Reporter James Elton has more from Katherine, which is still recovering from the floods earlier this month. title: What Aussies are sacrificing amid economic squeeze | ABC NEWS
description: The war in Iran has re-ignited cost-of-living concerns, with the treasurer warning inflation in Australia could surge to five per cent, driving up the cost of fuel, food and housing. The ABC asked Australians what they had given up to make ends meet as pressure on household budgets tightens. Among hundreds of responses, many described the difficulty of paying rent, insurance premiums and electricity costs, but food and groceries topped the survey. title: West Papua deforestation exposed in new documentary ‘Pesta Babi’ | The Pacific | ABC News
description: A new documentary, Pesta Babi (Pig Feast), investigates the rapid deforestation of West Papua and the forces driving it.   The film alleges Indonesia’s push for “food security” and “energy transition” is being used to justify large-scale agribusiness expansion, clearing millions of acres of forest and displacing Indigenous communities.   It also examines the role of the Indonesian military in supporting these projects. Filmmakers Dandhy Laksono and journalist Victor Mambor say telling this story has put them at personal risk.  ‘The Pacific’ is hosted by Johnson Raela and Alice Lolohea. #ABCThePacific  Watch The Pacific on ABC Australia or on ABC iView: https://iview.abc.net.au/show/pacific  Like ABC Pacific on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/ABCPacific  Follow ABC Pacific on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/abcpacific/  For more from ABC Pacific news, click here: https://www.abc.net.au/pacific   Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE Read more here:  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Hollywood action hero Chuck Norris dead at 86 | ABC NEWS
description: Hollywood action hero Chuck Norris has died at the age of 86 after a sudden hospitalisation. The martial artist was famous for his roles in Way Of The Dragon, Missing In Action, and Walker, Texas Ranger. His family said he had an "unwavering commitment to the people he loved". title: Asia faces an energy crunch, could China be the answer? | Asia News Week | ABC NEWS
description: China has called for an end to conflict in the Gulf and said the safety ‌of waterways should not be disturbed, adding that it was ready to work with Southeast Asia to address energy shortages.   Professor Stephen Nagy from the International Christian University in Tokyo talks to Asia News Week's Yvonne Yong about why China is not interested in having greater influence over Iran amid the conflict in the Middle East and whether Beijing is losing credibility as Tehran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz cripples global energy supplies.   Plus, he shares his analysis on the highly anticipated meeting between US president Donald Trump and Japanese prime minister Sanae Takaichi. title: Brisbane charity raided by police over 'From the River to the Sea' banner | ABC NEWS
description: Queensland Police have raided a Brisbane Catholic organisation over a banner that says: "From the River to the Sea, come get us Crisafulli."  On Friday morning, officers raided the Dorothy Day House, an informal Christian charity that provides food and housing to homeless people and refugees.  The Queensland government passed new laws this month that criminalise the use of the terms "From the River to the Sea" and "Globalise the Intifada".  Resident Franz Dowling said he created the banner to protest the law and its stifling effect on free speech.  Media organisations, such as the ABC, can legally use these terms in a journalistic context.  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Teens arrested after fire destroys Victorian ice-cream shop | ABC NEWS
description: The owner of an ice-cream store in Melbourne's west says he does not know why his shop was targeted after it was engulfed in flames.  Mahmoud, who did not provide his surname, said an alarm activated on his mobile phone at 2:30am, alerting him to a break-in at his store, Gelateria on the Docks, in Tarneit.  He said he checked his CCTV and saw someone inside the store before it erupted in fire.  "I don't know why they came and burned my shop," he said.  "I work very hard for this community. I've been open for two years.  "I need to ask the one who burnt my shop … why?"  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Rhoda Roberts, the woman behind 'Welcome to Country', dies at 66 | ABC NEWS
description: *WARNING: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander viewers are warned that this story includes images of an Indigenous person who has died. The ABC has been given permission to include her name and image. * A guiding force in Australia's arts scene and the woman who coined the term "Welcome to Country", Aunty Rhoda Roberts AO has died at the age of 66.  Roberts accomplished many firsts, including as the first Aboriginal host on mainstream television, the inaugural head of Indigenous programming at the Sydney Opera House and SBS's first Elder-in-residence.
